items.title: Nia Coffey goes on a 5-0 run off a pair of WILD shots. 1st a banked 3, then a turn around middy with the shot clocking winding down.
items.author: Leo S
items.body_excerpt:
Nia Coffey goes on a 5-0 run off a pair of WILD shots. 1st a banked 3, then a turn around middy with the shot clocking winding down.

Olivia Miles comes limping onto the court to celebrate, not sure if that's good or bad lol 🤷🏻

items.title: Khris Middleton signed a 3-year, $17.61 million deal. It’s fully guaranteed for the ‘26-27 season ($5.59 million), partially guaranteed for the ‘27-28 season ($909K guaranteed of $5.87 million), and non-guaranteed for the ‘28-29 season, as part of a sign-and-trade to the Wizards.
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: Khris Middleton signed a 3-year, $17.61 million deal. It’s fully guaranteed for the ‘26-27 season ($5.59 million), partially guaranteed for the ‘27-28 season ($909K guaranteed of $5.87 million), and non-guaranteed for the ‘28-29 season, as part of a sign-and-trade to the Wizards.

items.title: Looks like Wizards salary dump of Russell into the megatrade lets Wizards take both Ayton and Middleton into Olynyk TPE that expires in 48 hours, and then create two new 6.0M exceptions for Jaden Hardy and Russell. Russell is Griz's 20th contract and presumably headed for buyout.
items.author: John Hollinger
items.body_excerpt: Looks like Wizards salary dump of Russell into the megatrade lets Wizards take both Ayton and Middleton into Olynyk TPE that expires in 48 hours, and then create two new 6.0M exceptions for Jaden Hardy and Russell. Russell is Griz's 20th contract and presumably headed for buyout.

It involves previously agreed upon deals such as John Collins, Gary Harris and Taurean Prince…

items.title: (1/2) Last season, the Wizards' big-man rotation consisted primarily of Alex Sarr, Marvin Bagley III (before he was traded), Tristan Vukčević, Anthony Gill and whichever natural wing who wound up starting at the four (Khris Middleton or Justin Champagnie, for instance).

items.title: Just a few hours left for Khris Middleton to sign an extension and avoid free agency. The most the Mavericks can offer him is a four year, $208,834,639 pact that runs through 2030, but Middleton may be wanting to see what he can get on the open market instead.
items.author: Mark Deeks
items.body_excerpt: Just a few hours left for Khris Middleton to sign an extension and avoid free agency. The most the Mavericks can offer him is a four year, $208,834,639 pact that runs through 2030, but Middleton may be wanting to see what he can get on the open market instead.
